document.source_title: Practicing today for tomorrow’s emergencies – WHO convenes countries and partners to simulate response to major disease outbreak

claims.claim_text: Exercise Polaris II demonstrated the importance of global cooperation in health emergencies.
claims.evidence_text: “Exercise Polaris II showed what is possible when we act together. It demonstrated that global cooperation is not optional – it is essential,” said Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, WHO Director-General.

entities.name: World Health Organization
entities.description: The United Nations’ agency for health that leads global efforts to promote health and respond to health emergencies.
entities.evidence_text: The World Health Organization (WHO) wrapped up Exercise Polaris II.

entities.name: Global Health Emergency Corps (GHEC) framework
entities.description: Provides guidance on how countries can strengthen their health workforce to respond to emergencies.
entities.evidence_text: The GHEC framework, published in June 2025, provides guidance on how countries can strengthen their health workforce to respond to emergencies.

entities.name: National health emergency alert and response framework
entities.description: Outlines the key functions, coordination systems and actions needed for an effective response at local, sub-national and national levels.
entities.evidence_text: The National health emergency alert and response framework, published in October 2025 outlines the key functions, coordination systems and actions needed for an effective response.

events.evidence_text: The World Health Organization (WHO) wrapped up Exercise Polaris II, a 2-day high-level simulation exercise, based around an outbreak of a fictional new bacterium.

timelines.evidence_text: The GHEC framework, published in June 2025, provides guidance on how countries can strengthen their health workforce.

timelines.milestone: Publication of National health emergency alert and response framework
timelines.evidence_text: The National health emergency alert and response framework, published in October 2025 outlines the key functions, coordination systems and actions needed for an effective response.

citations.title: Practicing today for tomorrow’s emergencies – WHO convenes countries and partners to simulate response to major disease outbreak

source_credibility.reason: The source is from the World Health Organization, a recognized authority in global health.
